page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 4 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a total of 160 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.2 GHz, supported by a 38 MB L3 cache. It includes 340.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.08 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is weak, as evidenced by bottom-tier results in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. However, the server excels in GPU-driven tasks, achieving top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as top-tier text generation speeds for medium and large models.

Given the high density of dedicated GPU resources and system memory, this server represents a specialized hardware class where cost efficiency is realized through accelerated parallel processing rather than general-purpose CPU compute. It is highly optimized for deep learning, large language model inference, and data science workloads that can leverage the four NVIDIA A100 GPUs. It is less suitable for single-threaded CPU tasks or applications requiring high local storage capacity.
description: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB of total VRAM, paired with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 340.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance does not include local storage. Benchmark results highlight a distinct tradeoff: while single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are weak, the server delivers top-tier performance in large language model inference prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain average. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for parallelized, GPU-intensive workloads such as deep learning, AI inference, and complex data science tasks, whereas it is poorly suited for single-threaded CPU operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 340GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g features 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 340GB RAM, optimized for high-performance AI and LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring four dedicated GPUs and high-capacity memory for demanding machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM
48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 340 GB system memory
Top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
Weak single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for deep learning, AI inference, and parallel data science workloads
